body: PT Cahaya Anagata Energi, a joint venture between PT Pertamina Geothermal Energy and Chevron New Energies Holdings Indonesia, has secured a geothermal license for the Way Ratai geothermal work area in Lampung, marking a significant step towards exploring renewable energy resources. The partnership aims to enhance the geothermal portfolio and generate major benefits for the public, with a commitment to transparency and collaboration with stakeholders, including surrounding communities.
title: PT Cahaya Anagata Energi Secures Geothermal License for Way Ratai Work Area in Lampung

body: PT Hexindo Adiperkasa Tbk will allocate a cash dividend of USD 38.99 million, or USD 0.0464 per share, which represents 70% of its net profit of USD 55.71 million for the year ended March 31, 2024. The dividend payment is scheduled for October 25, 2024, following a decline in revenue to USD 612.32 million in March 2024, but with increases in gross profit and operating profit.
title: PT Hexindo Adiperkasa to Distribute USD 38.99 Million in Cash Dividends to Shareholders

body: PT Waskita Beton Precast Tbk has paid IDR 84.57 billion for its cash flow available debt services (CFADS) Phase IV, consisting of coupon bond payments, interest rates, and principal payments to bank creditors and trade creditors. This payment follows the company's previous CFADS Phase III payment of IDR 76.43 billion in March 2024, with the next scheduled payment due on March 25, 2025.
title: PT Waskita Beton Precast pays IDR 84.57 billion in cash flow available debt services for Phase IV.

body: PT Golden Westindo Arta Jaya Tbk has postponed its initial public offering (IPO), which was initially scheduled for September 27 to October 1, 2024, after conducting book building from September 10-18, 2024, with 685.71 million shares offered at a price range of IDR 100-120 per share. Assuming an offering price of IDR 120 per share, the company could potentially raise IDR 82.28 billion in fresh funds from the IPO.
title: PT Golden Westindo Arta Jaya Tbk postpones initial public offering scheduled for September 27 to October 1, 2024.

body: PT Bank Mestika Dharma Tbk plans to transfer 63.02 million treasury shares on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, which were acquired through a buyback program from April 17 to June 12, 2020, at an average price of IDR 1,095 per share, totaling IDR 69.02 billion. The proceeds from the share transfer will be used as extra working capital, and management assures that the transaction will not negatively impact the company's performance.
title: PT Bank Mestika Dharma to Transfer 63 Million Treasury Shares on Indonesia Stock Exchange

body: PT Astra Agro Lestari Tbk will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 161.67 billion, or IDR 84 per share, to its shareholders, following approval from the board of commissioners on September 24, 2024. The company reported a net profit of IDR 642.94 billion as of August 31, 2024, and retained earnings of IDR 17.35 trillion.
title: Astra Agro Lestari to Distribute IDR 161.67 Billion Interim Dividend to Shareholders

body: PT Multitrend Indo TBK's (Baby) stock price plummeted 27.88% to IDR 208 per share, with a market capitalization of IDR 544.17 billion, a year after its IPO. Meanwhile, PT Anugerah Spareparts Sejahtera TBK's (AEGS) stock price dropped 64% to IDR 36 per share, with a market capitalization of IDR 36.21 billion, also a year after its IPO.
title: Two Indonesian Companies, PT Multitrend Indo and PT Anugerah Spareparts Sejahtera, See Sharp Decline in Stock Prices One Year After IPO.

body: PT Majukarya Mandiri Indonesia reduced its stake in PT Buana Lintas Lautan TBK to 5.07% by selling 83.58 million shares, while PT Shima Global Kapital increased its holdings in PT Energi Mega Persada TBK to 21.81% by acquiring 34.55 million shares. Other notable transactions include PT Sinar Mas Cakrawala acquiring 2.55 million shares of PT Sinar Mas Multiartha TBK and PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa TBK buying back 140,900 shares.
title: Multiple investors make changes to their shareholdings in various Indonesian companies.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 4.43 billion shares, with significant selling activity in telecommunications company PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk and digital corporation PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, among others. Meanwhile, foreign investors purchased a significant number of shares in companies such as PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk, and PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk, among others, with net buying volumes ranging from 64.40 million to 173.99 million shares.
title: Foreign investors sell more shares than they buy in Indonesian market, with net foreign volume closing at -4.43 billion shares.

body: Prinsip Management Limited (PML) divested 18.93 million shares of PT FAP Agri Tbk at IDR 5,300 each, collecting IDR 100.36 billion and reducing its stake from 75.28% to 74.75% of total listed shares. The transaction volume reached 1,700 shares worth IDR 9.01 billion, with the stock price hovering around IDR 5,200 per share during the divestment process.
title: Prinsip Management Limited divests 18.93 million shares of PT Fap Agri TBK, reducing its stake to 74.75% from 75.28%.
